summary: EPIC is structured to satisfy the needs of upper-intermediate students. EPIC is a package of six language-focused courses (four skills-based courses and two applied communication courses) taken over one semester to ensure a high degree of intensity in English study. Each of these courses is further subdivided into project-based modules. EPIC covers language knowledge, all four language skills, and a variety of applied activities and projects. Each of the three EPIC instructors are responsible for four modules during the semester and students receive instruction in the full range of English competencies. Extensive Reading (ER) and self-access learning are built into the EPIC Program to encourage greater learner autonomy in language study. Three native-speaker instructors closely coordinate curriculum, course material, and assessment.
aims: Students work on improving language proficiency with an emphasis on using language accurately and appropriately, developing both vocabulary and syntax and the ability to use language in context. In the spoken language course (15 lessons), students work on improving skills of small-group conversation, presentations and debate. By the end of the course, students will be able to demonstrate advanced levels of English proficiency.

prereq: Students are selected for EPIC based on first-year English results or English ability. A speaking and writing assessment will be done in the first two classes of the EPIC Programme to determine eligibility to take this special course. As an advanced level English-medium program, students will have to demonstrate the English writing and speaking skills required for engaging in all course content. Students must also demonstrate motivation required for a course based on intensity of study. Students who are interested in EPIC should consult with the General Education administrative staff or Prof. Darren Lingley prior to enrollment. There is a special pre-registration procedure for enrollment in EPIC and information for this will be provided prior to the second semester.
prep: Students should keep a folder of work proving they have done all the preparation for all project work (spoken activities and all written assignments). Your folder should show that you do self-motivated study in addition to the tasks set by the EPIC teacher. Students will be expected to do a significant amount of work outside of class hours to successfully complete this special intensive EPIC Programme.
teachers: ◎ ダレン リングリー
